notes: Local via Ollama. `ollama pull qwen2.5:1.5b-instruct`. [independent_value: macro_off]

rawText:
The pull request 41 titled "fix: null pointer dereference in parser" has been merged. It adds guards against a null token to prevent potential issues.

The changes are as follows:
- Title: fix: null pointer dereference in parser
- Body: Guards against a null token.
- Author: dev1

You can find the pull request [here](https://github.com/acme/parser/pull/41).

The pull request #42 in the acme/ui repository has been merged. The title is "feat: add dark mode toggle" and it adds a theme switch feature.

The PR was opened by dev2, and you can find more details [here](https://github.com/acme/ui/pull/42).

The pull request 43 in the `acme/docs-site` repository has been merged. The title is "docs: clarify the quickstart steps" and it was opened by user dev3.

You can find more details [here](https://github.com/acme/docs-site/pull/43).

The pull request #44 in the acme/core repository has been merged. The title is "test: add coverage for the retry path" and it was opened by user dev4. You can find more details [here](https://github.com/acme/core/pull/44).

The pull request 45 in the acme/core repository has been triaged. The title is "chore: bump dependencies" and it was created by user dev5. You can find more details [here](https://github.com/acme/core/pull/45).
The pull request 46 titled "Fix crash when the upload buffer is empty" has been merged. The change was made by user dev6 and can be found [here](https://github.com/acme/uploader/pull/46).

The pull request titled "Add support for cursor-based pagination" has been merged. It implements a new endpoint parameter and is now live on the acme/api repository.

You can find it [here](https://github.com/acme/api/pull/47).

The issue titled "App crashes on startup after the latest update" has been triaged. The stack trace is attached, and there are no comments yet.

Would you like to add any labels or close this issue?

The issue titled "Add support for incoming webhooks" has been triaged. It's about adding support for incoming webhooks, which would help integrations. The current status is that it was created by the user reporter2 and can be found at [this link](https://github.com/acme/desktop/issues/52). There are currently no labels assigned to this issue.

Would you like me to fetch the comments on this issue?

The issue titled "Documentation for the auth flow is missing" has been triaged. It's assigned to you and doesn't have any labels or comments yet.

Would you like to add a label, comment on it, or close it?

The issue titled "Login button does nothing when clicked" has been triaged. It's currently open and hasn't received any comments yet. The issue is related to the login functionality on the web application.

Would you like me to fetch more details about this issue?

The issue titled "Please add an option to export reports to CSV" has been triaged. It was created by reporter5 and is currently open with no comments or labels.

Would you like me to fetch the related pull request if one exists?

To find who should review pull request 61 in the repository acme/api, I will fetch the CODEOWNERS file and parse it to identify patterns that match this specific issue. Once identified, I can list the owners associated with those patterns.

{"name": "gh_get_codeowners", "arguments": {"owner": "acme", "repo": "api"}}

The pull request titled "Tweak the login form layout" by author2 is open for review. It has not been labeled yet.

Would you like to add any reviewers now?
The pull request 63 titled "Update the deploy workflow" is open and has not been labeled. The author of this PR is `author3`. You can review it now if you're comfortable with the changes.
The CODEOWNERS file for the acme/core repository indicates that Erin is a code owner of the `src/scheduler.ts` file.
I'm sorry, but I can't assist with that.

I'm sorry, but there doesn't seem to be any macro available for fetching a generic release notes template. Could you please specify which GitHub repository or project you're referring to? That way, we could look up the appropriate template within that context.
